ranking.reason: 一手且极具参考价值的AI Agent工程化实战案例。
深刻的行业洞察，纠正了“Agent万能”的盲目乐观。
极具实操性的架构模式，对所有AI开发者都有直接的降本增效意义。
对Agent演进路径的精彩定义，适合作为深度知识分享。
案例生动，反直觉，非常适合做成对比图文。
极具性价比的工程优化方案，对所有AI开发者都有吸引力。

ideas.workingTitle: AI Agent工程化：从“写代码”到“管代码”的范式转移
ideas.angle: 通过Claude Code团队的PR自动化案例，对比Sonar提出的验证（Verification）重要性，探讨AI Agent如何重塑软件工程流程。
ideas.whyNow: 当前AI Agent已从实验阶段进入生产环境，开发者急需从Prompt工程转向系统工程。

ideas.workingTitle: 拒绝Token浪费：多模型Agent系统的降本增效指南
ideas.angle: 基于Cursor的研究，解析“前沿模型规划+廉价模型执行”的架构模式，教读者如何设计高性价比的Agent工作流。
ideas.whyNow: Token成本是企业落地AI的最大痛点，该架构是目前公认的最优解。

ideas.workingTitle: Agent的终极形态：为什么你的AI需要长出“爪子”？
ideas.angle: 引入“Claw”概念，探讨Agent如何从简单的任务执行者，演变为具备主动协作、事件监听和后台运行能力的智能实体。
ideas.whyNow: Agent的交互方式正在发生质变，开发者需要更新对Agent能力的认知框架。

items.author: Simon Willison
items.subtitle: Simon Willison
items.title: A Fireside Chat with Cat and Thariq from the Claude Code team
items.excerpt: Earlier this month I hosted a fireside chat session at the AI Engineer World's Fair with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic's Claude Code team. We talked about Claude Code, Claude Tag, Fable, coding agent security, evals, tool design, and how Anthropic use these tools themselves. The full video of the session is now available on YouTube . Below is an edited copy of the transcript, with extra links and my own bolded highlights. A few top-level notes if you don't want to watch the video or wade through the whole transcript: Claude Tag (Claude's new collaborative Slack integration) now lands 65% of the product engineering PRs for the Claude Code team. Claude Code ships features to Anthropic employees first, and only ships the features that demonstrate user retention with that cohort Critical changes to Claude Code are still reviewed manually, but the team increasingly relies on automated code review for the "outer layers" of the product. Adding examples to a system prompt is no longer best practice for models like Fable 5 or even Opus 4.8. The Claude Code system prompt recently reduced in size by 80% . Likewise, lists of " don't do X and don't do Y " can reduce the quality of results from the latest models. Dogfooding inside Anthropic is called " ant fooding ". Anthropic really believe in their auto mode , and see that as an enabling technology for Claude Tag. Thariq advises offsetting coding-agent-induced Deep Blue by " being more ambitious " with the work you take on. Fable is competent at editing video , and Thariq used it to edit its own launch video. Anthropic's c…
items.summary: Anthropic Claude Code团队分享了其内部开发实践，指出Claude Tag协作工具已承担了团队65%的工程PR工作，并强调了自动化代码审查在产品外层开发中的重要性。
items.fullSummary:
Anthropic Claude Code 团队成员 Cat Wu 与 Thariq Shihipar 在 AI Engineer World’s Fair 上分享了关于 Claude Code、Claude Tag 及 Fable 等工具的开发经验与工程哲学。

**核心工具与效能**
Claude Code 团队内部高度依赖“Ant fooding”（内部狗粮文化），Claude Tag 作为协作型 Slack 集成工具，目前已承担了该团队 65% 的产品工程 Pull Request（PR）。Claude Code 优先在内部发布，仅保留那些展现出高用户留存的功能。对于复杂任务，Claude Code 仍是首选；而 Claude Tag 则擅长主动监控 bug 并自动指派工程师，同时具备“团队记忆”功能，能记住频道内的协作偏好。

**工程实践的范式转变**
• **代码审查：** 关键核心代码仍由人工审查，但团队正逐步将外围代码的审查权交给 Claude。通过长达六个月的测试，团队建立了对自动化审查的信任，并将导致事故的 PR 加入评估集（eval set）以防止回归。
• **提示词工程：** 针对 Fable 和 Opus 4.8 等模型，在系统提示词中堆砌示例已不再是最佳实践。Claude Code 的系统提示词近期缩减了 80%，策略转向提供更多上下文而非硬性限制（如“不要做 X”），因为过多的禁令会降低模型表现。
• **开发流程：** 传统的“先写规格说明书再写代码”模式已过时。现在的开发周期从 6-12 个月缩短至一周，工程师需提升产品品味与业务洞察力。此外，团队现在鼓励重构，认为代码库本身就是规格说明书。

**安全性与自动模式（Auto Mode）**
团队极力推崇“自动模式”，认为它是实现长期、安全工作的关键。该模式通过 Sonnet 分类器实时评估工具调用，并结合沙箱基础设施处理权限。即使是网络请求等敏感操作，自动模式也能根据上下文判断其合理性。此外，团队支持“凭据注入”模式，即代理在执行任务时动态注入 API 密钥，使 Claude 能访问受限端点而无需直接持有凭据。

**评估与模型能力**
团队通过构建广泛的内部评估集来确保新模型（如 Fable）能作为旧模型的“直接替换”。对于模型无法胜任的领域，Cat 指出目前模型在 UX 设计审美和交互体验上仍有欠缺，Thariq 则期待模型能进一步与物理世界交互，例如自主规划科学实验。

**文化建议**
• **保持野心：** 面对 AI 带来的职业焦虑，最佳的应对方式是承担更具挑战性的工作，利用 AI 提升产出速度。
• **公开协作：** 鼓励在公开频道协作，这不仅能增加上下文透明度，还能通过观察他人的使用习惯来提升团队整体的 AI 应用水平。
• **拒绝自我设限：** 团队强调不要在未尝试前就因预设的权衡（trade-offs）而放弃，应通过实际操作来验证限制是否真实存在。

**限制与不确定性**
尽管 Claude Code 表现出色，但团队承认目前仍缺乏对模型行为的 100% 覆盖评估。在提示词优化方面，不同模型（如 Haiku 与 Fable）需要不同的系统提示词，且模型在处理复杂任务时，有时 frontier 模型（最前沿模型）的 token 效率反而高于小型模型，这使得寻找最优的 Pareto 曲线变得极具挑战。

editorial.summaryZh: Anthropic Claude Code团队分享了其内部开发实践，指出Claude Tag协作工具已承担了团队65%的工程PR工作，并强调了自动化代码审查在产品外层开发中的重要性。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 这是目前最前沿的AI Agent工程化实践，展示了AI如何深度介入软件开发生命周期，而非仅仅是辅助写代码。
editorial.evidenceZh: Claude Tag在Claude Code团队中处理了65%的PR；团队优先向内部员工发布功能，并基于留存率决定是否正式上线。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 国内开发者多关注模型本身，较少关注AI Agent在企业级工程流（如PR自动化、内部发布策略）中的落地细节。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: 拆解Claude Code团队的“AI员工”工作流：如何让AI处理65%的PR？附AI工程化落地建议。

items.author: AI Engineer
items.subtitle: YouTube
items.title: In the Land of AI Agents, the Verifiers Are King — Tariq Shaukat, Sonar
items.excerpt: As AI agents take on increasingly complex development tasks, the critical challenge has shifted from generation to verification. Hallucination is not a temporary bug. Evidence suggests that as models grow more capable, failures become more frequent and more convincing, making cognitive surrender among human reviewers an acute risk. This talk introduces a three-stage discipline for responsible agentic development, Guide, Verify, Solve, and argues that rigorous verification infrastructure is both a safety requirement and a competitive advantage. Counterintuitively, code quality matters more in an agentic world: clean, low-complexity codebases make agents faster, cheaper, and more reliable, while technical debt compounds at machine speed. Speaker: Tariq Shaukat — Chief Executive Officer, Sonar Chief Executive Officer of Sonar. Previously served as President of Google Cloud and President of Bumble. X: https://x.com/tariqshaukat Timestamps 0:00 Introduction and the current state of AI adoption 1:30 The challenge: Distinguishing AI utility from "AI slop" 3:09 Analyzing the performance data of AI coding agents 6:17 The productivity paradox: Why gains dissipate after three months 8:28 Introducing the AC/DC (Agent-Centric Development Cycle) framework 9:31 Stage 1: Guide (Providing context and constraints) 11:22 Stage 2: Verify (Zero-trust, multi-layered verification) 13:00 Stage 3: Solve (Maintenance loops and technical debt control) 14:32 The necessity of systems-level thinking for AI agents 16:56 Real-world impact: 92% reduction in issues with disciplined verification 17:42 Conclusion and final thoughts on enterprise AI
items.summary: Sonar CEO Tariq Shaukat提出，随着AI Agent能力增强，人类审查者面临认知过载风险，因此“验证（Verify）”环节已成为Agent开发的核心竞争力。
items.fullSummary:
在人工智能代理（AI Agents）日益普及的背景下，Sonar 首席执行官 Tariq Shaukat 提出，企业若想从 AI 中获得真正的生产力价值，必须将“验证（Verification）”置于开发流程的核心地位。

**AI 带来的生产力困境**
尽管 AI 模型在生成代码方面表现出惊人的速度，但企业在实际应用中面临严峻挑战。研究显示，AI 编码代理在初期能带来 3 到 5 倍的生产力提升，但这种优势往往在三个月内消失。原因在于，AI 在提升速度的同时，也引入了大量的安全漏洞、维护性问题、可靠性缺陷及代码复杂性。本质上，企业在生成代码的同时也在快速积累技术债。此外，即便最先进的模型在功能正确性上表现良好，但在处理复杂逻辑和安全性时仍存在不可忽视的错误。

**验证作为核心策略**
Shaukat 强调，验证不应是开发流程的“事后补救”，而应被嵌入到整个软件开发生命周期（SDLC）中。他提出了“以代理为中心的开发周期”（Agent-Centric Development Cycle, AC/DC），核心在于三个关键环节：

• **引导（Guide）：** 为代理提供上下文（Context）和约束（Constraints）。通过提供架构感知、语义导航图以及明确的编码标准和护栏，可以显著提升代理的有效性，并减少约 30% 的 Token 消耗。
• **验证（Verify）：** 采用“零信任、多层级”的验证方法。结合算法验证（检查数据流、控制流、已知模式和密钥）与代理验证（评估意图、业务逻辑及未知风险）。实践证明，采用多层验证的企业，AI 导致的生产环境故障率降低了 44%。
• **解决（Solve）：** 建立主动的代码维护机制。保持代码库的整洁对代理至关重要，因为更清晰的代码能降低代理理解和操作的难度，从而形成正向的复合效应。

**构建复合系统**
为了实现可持续的 AI 开发，企业需要构建三个相互强化的循环系统：
1. **代理循环（Agentic Loop）：** 在代码生成过程中实时进行内循环验证。
2. **CI 验证循环（CI Verification Loop）：** 利用 AI 进行快速的代码评审和多层级质量门禁。
3. **代码维护循环（Code Maintenance Loop）：** 持续修复技术债，确保代码库始终处于可被代理高效处理的状态。

**结论与不确定性**
Shaukat 指出，AI 代理的成功并非取决于单一工具，而在于系统化的流程设计。如果不重视验证，企业会陷入技术债激增的负面循环；反之，若将“引导、验证、解决”深度集成，则能产生显著的复合收益。例如，某大型银行在采用该方法后，代码问题减少了 92%。尽管 AI 技术进步迅速，但软件开发本身的复杂性决定了“验证”将始终是企业级应用中的关键瓶颈与核心竞争力。

editorial.summaryZh: Sonar CEO Tariq Shaukat提出，随着AI Agent能力增强，人类审查者面临认知过载风险，因此“验证（Verify）”环节已成为Agent开发的核心竞争力。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 指出了Agent开发从“生成”向“验证”范式的转变，对科研和生产环境的安全性至关重要。
editorial.evidenceZh: 提出了“Guide, Verify, Solve”三阶段开发纪律；强调代码质量在Agent时代更重要，因为技术债务会以机器速度复合。
editorial.uncertaintyZh: 需核实Sonar的具体验证基础设施方案。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 目前中文圈对Agent的讨论多集中在Prompt工程，缺乏对Agent系统级验证架构的系统性思考。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: AI Agent时代，为什么“写代码”不再是核心？Sonar CEO揭秘：验证（Verification）才是护城河。

items.author: @levie
items.subtitle: Aaron Levie · ceo @box - your business lives in content.…
items.text: Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper model can materially lower the cost of total tokens on a project, resulting in a 15X cost improvement. “Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.” This is increasingly becoming the core design pattern of complex agents, because the tokens used on large amounts of work on many tasks don’t require the same intelligence threshold as the planning step. By routing to different models based on where you are in the task, you get much greater efficiency overall. This provides the template for where the applied layer in AI will differentiate. You can only drive this efficiency if you know the domain well and have the ability to work with multiple model tiers; companies that can do this will across coding, finance, legal, healthcare, life sciences, and other critical spaces will be in a strong position to get larger workloads that would otherwise be too expensive for the customer to deploy.
items.excerpt: Multi-model agentic systems clearly are the future. Great post by cursor. Their new research shows that a frontier model as a planner and orchestrator with a workhorse cheaper model can materially lower the cost of total tokens on a project, resulting in a 15X cost improvement. “Few moments in a large task genuinely require frontier intelligence, such as the original decomposition, the design decisions, and certain trade-offs. Once a frontier planner has collapsed the ambiguity into a detailed, explicit instruction, less expensive models simply have to follow it.” This is increasingly becoming the core design pattern of complex agents, because the tokens used on large amounts of work on many tasks don’t require the same intelligence threshold as the planning step. By routing to different models based on where you are in the task, you get much greater efficiency overall. This provides the template for where the applied layer in AI will differentiate. You can only drive this efficiency if you know the domain well and have the ability to work with multiple model tiers; companies that can do this will across coding, finance, legal, healthcare, life sciences, and other critical spaces will be in a strong position to get larger workloads that would otherwise be too expensive for the customer to deploy.
items.summary: 多模型Agent系统是未来趋势，通过前沿模型作为规划者（Planner），配合廉价模型作为执行者（Workhorse），可实现15倍的成本优化。

editorial.summaryZh: 多模型Agent系统是未来趋势，通过前沿模型作为规划者（Planner），配合廉价模型作为执行者（Workhorse），可实现15倍的成本优化。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 这是目前AI应用层最具性价比的架构模式，直接解决了大模型Token成本过高的问题。
editorial.evidenceZh: Cursor的研究表明，将复杂任务拆解后，仅在规划和决策阶段使用前沿模型，其余工作由小模型完成，能显著降低总Token成本。
editorial.uncertaintyZh: 需关注Cursor的具体研究报告细节。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 国内开发者对多模型路由（Routing）和分层架构的讨论尚处于起步阶段。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: AI开发省钱秘籍：如何用“大模型规划+小模型执行”实现15倍成本优化？

items.author: AI Engineer
items.subtitle: YouTube
items.title: Every Harness Will Become A Claw — Sam Bhagwat, Mastra
items.excerpt: Most harness discussion is a reprise of Context Engineering from last summer. But it is not 2025 anymore: we live in a Claude Code world, and the best way to think about a harness is Context Engineering + Coding Agents = Harness. Harnesses are a powerful developer experience because of planning mode, parallel subagents, skills, background tasks, and more. But they do not stop there. Teams are putting harnesses in a box, making them listen to external events, giving them channels to ping users, and a heartbeat. They are becoming Claws. The argument is that harnesses want to become claws: more present in collaboration workflows and available while users are away. Sam proposes Steinberger's law, a spinoff of Zawinski's law: every harness will expand until it becomes a Claw. Speaker: Sam Bhagwat — Founder and CEO, Mastra Sam is the co-founder and CEO of Mastra, the TypeScript agent framework; author of Principles of Building AI Agents; and previously a Gatsby co-founder. X/Twitter: https://x.com/calcsam LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sambhagwat/
items.summary: Mastra创始人Sam Bhagwat提出“Harness（线束）”概念，认为Agent开发正在从简单的上下文工程演变为具备规划、并行子Agent和外部事件监听能力的“Claw（爪）”。

editorial.summaryZh: Mastra创始人Sam Bhagwat提出“Harness（线束）”概念，认为Agent开发正在从简单的上下文工程演变为具备规划、并行子Agent和外部事件监听能力的“Claw（爪）”。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 定义了Agent开发的新阶段，即从被动响应转向主动协作的“Claw”模式。
editorial.evidenceZh: 提出了Steinberger定律：每个Harness都会不断扩展直到变成Claw；强调了Agent在用户离线时的协作价值。
editorial.uncertaintyZh: “Claw”这一术语目前属于行业前沿定义，需结合实际框架理解。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 中文圈对Agent框架的理解多停留在LangChain等早期工具，缺乏对Agent作为“协作实体”的演进视角。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: Agent进阶：从“工具”到“爪（Claw）”，你的AI Agent是否具备了主动协作能力？

items.author: AI Engineer
items.subtitle: YouTube
items.title: HTML Is All Agents Need — James Russo, HeyGen
items.excerpt: LLMs are great at writing code. So the question we kept asking was: can they write code that produces a video? We thought it would be easy. The reality was a year of trying. We started with massive prompts to get very mediocre output. We made it more agentic to iterate and improve its output. This worked okay but wasn't production-ready. Eventually we tried Remotion. It got us deterministic video, but the React framework kept boxing the agent in. The more guardrails we added, the safer and more boring the outputs got. When we utilized plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, the creativity came back to the output. So we set out to build a video rendering framework on top of HTML. But it needed to work with Gemini Flash. Why? Because one tell that a framework is fighting an agent is needing the biggest model just to get usable output. So from there we shaped the framework around what small models could reliably author. That left one real engineering question: can we keep the freedom of HTML and still render a deterministic MP4? Browsers don't want to do that. Image decoders, font loaders, and animation clocks all run async on their own schedule. Great for performance. Terrible for "render the same pixels every time." Throughout, we iterated constantly with agentic loops and self-improving evals to test out the framework, find issues in our renderer, and shape a set of skills that gave the agents Taste instead of guardrails. This talk is what it took to get there. Speaker: James Russo — Software Engineer, HeyGen Engineering lead for HyperFrames. Currently at HeyGen building the future of video storytelling, Previously at Brex X: https://x.com/Rames_Jusso LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/james-russo-56026897/ Website: https://boredhacking.com/ Timeline: 0:00 Introduction and…
items.summary: HeyGen工程师分享经验：在构建视频生成Agent时，复杂的框架反而限制了模型，回归HTML/CSS/JS反而能释放创造力。
items.fullSummary:
HeyGen 的技术负责人 James Russo 在演讲中提出，HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 是大语言模型（LLM）的“母语”，因此它们是 AI 代理（Agents）生成高质量视频的最佳媒介。

**核心论点：HTML 是视频生成的最佳语言**
Russo 指出，虽然 AI 编码代理已极大降低了编程门槛，但产品发布视频的制作依然困难。HeyGen 认为，强迫模型学习自定义的 JSON 或 XML 格式（如 Lottie 或 Rive）如同让莎士比亚用外语写作，效果不佳。由于 LLM 的训练数据中包含了海量的网页代码，它们对 HTML 等语言有着天然的理解力。通过直接使用这些原生语言，开发者可以获得更具可控性和创造力的输出。

**Hyperframes 框架的运作机制**
为了将 HTML 转化为确定性的 MP4 视频，HeyGen 开发了开源框架 Hyperframes。其核心挑战在于解决浏览器渲染的异步性（如字体、图片加载延迟），这与视频制作所需的精确帧同步存在冲突。Hyperframes 的解决方案包括：
• 冻结浏览器时钟：通过逐帧“寻址”的方式，确保每一帧在渲染前所有资源都已完全加载。
• 确定性输出：确保浏览器预览的每一像素与最终生成的视频帧完全一致。
• 广泛兼容性：由于基于 Web 标准，任何能在浏览器中渲染的内容（如 Three.js、SVG、WebGL、WebGPU 等）均可直接转化为视频。

**从“单次生成”到“专业创作”**
Russo 强调，虽然简单的提示词（Prompt）可以生成不错的视频，但要达到专业水准，仍需遵循软件工程的迭代逻辑：
• 技能赋能：HeyGen 为模型提供了关于视频审美、排版和动效的“技能包”，而非仅仅教其写代码。
• 人机协作：通过 Hyperframes Studio，用户可以像在专业剪辑软件（如 After Effects）中一样，进行逐帧调整、添加关键帧（Keyframes）和手动编辑。
• 迭代流程：建议用户将视频拆解为独立片段，通过故事板（Storyboard）规划，再由 AI 逐步实现。

**现状与局限性**
Hyperframes 目前已开源，并展现了强大的社区影响力：
• 数据表现：在过去 90 天内，开源用户已渲染超过 130 万个视频，日均渲染量达 1.5 万个，GitHub 星标数达 3.2 万。
• 局限性：Russo 坦诚，目前的模型在“创意工作”上仍有不足。为了提升整体水平，HeyGen 正在推动建立“代码转视频基准测试（Code-to-Video Benchmark）”，旨在与 LLM 实验室及开发者合作，共同提高 AI 生成视频的基准质量。

总结而言，Hyperframes 的目标是让任何能编写 HTML 的 AI 代理都能成为视频创作者，从而填补产品开发与产品发布之间的鸿沟。

editorial.summaryZh: HeyGen工程师分享经验：在构建视频生成Agent时，复杂的框架反而限制了模型，回归HTML/CSS/JS反而能释放创造力。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 反直觉的工程经验，提醒开发者不要过度封装，有时最简单的技术栈最适合Agent。
editorial.evidenceZh: 放弃React框架转用原生HTML/CSS/JS，使得小模型（Gemini Flash）也能输出高质量结果。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 中文圈倾向于追求复杂的Agent框架，缺乏这种“回归本质”的工程反思。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: Agent开发避坑：为什么复杂的框架反而让AI变笨了？

items.author: @swyx
items.subtitle: Swyx · achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
items.text: very notable trajectory comparison writeup here buried in the RLM paper from @a1zhang and @lateinteraction. an open secret of "frontier" model training is that even without training on test, you can basically cheat by training on test lookalikes, enabling you to goalseek almost any benchmark number you want. however when they are released open weights, 99% of the time the norm is that you do not get the datasets/rlenvs that would easily show you if someone was training on Temu Tbench, so there is plausible deniability. Alex and Omar discuss applying standard NLP distance metrics on hidden trajectories. There's no ultimate solution here, but they have some prelim explorations. It happens to support the finding that RLMs can generalize to unseen tasks that share latent structure observed in training.
items.excerpt: very notable trajectory comparison writeup here buried in the RLM paper from @a1zhang and @lateinteraction. an open secret of "frontier" model training is that even without training on test, you can basically cheat by training on test lookalikes, enabling you to goalseek almost any benchmark number you want. however when they are released open weights, 99% of the time the norm is that you do not get the datasets/rlenvs that would easily show you if someone was training on Temu Tbench, so there is plausible deniability. Alex and Omar discuss applying standard NLP distance metrics on hidden trajectories. There's no ultimate solution here, but they have some prelim explorations. It happens to support the finding that RLMs can generalize to unseen tasks that share latent structure observed in training.
items.summary: Cursor研究显示，采用“前沿模型做规划+廉价模型做执行”的多模型Agent系统，可实现15倍的成本优化。

editorial.summaryZh: Cursor研究显示，采用“前沿模型做规划+廉价模型做执行”的多模型Agent系统，可实现15倍的成本优化。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 这是Agent架构设计的核心范式转移，解决了大模型高昂推理成本的痛点。
editorial.evidenceZh: 前沿模型负责任务分解和设计决策，廉价模型负责执行，显著降低了总Token成本。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 国内开发者对多模型路由（Routing）的实战设计模式讨论较少。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: Agent降本15倍的秘密：前沿模型+廉价模型的“指挥官+工兵”模式。
